intent: Define the SecurityRecipes MCP and agentic-skill risk coverage profile: a machine-readable map from the current OWASP MCP Top 10 beta and OWASP Agentic Skills Top 10 into SecurityRecipes secure-context, MCP authorization, connector trust, agent skill, telemetry, incident response, and runtime action evidence.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Product Security, AppSec, Security Engineering, GRC, procurement security, model-risk, agent-host, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.category: MCP and agentic-skill risk coverage for the secure context layer
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes can show, in one generated packet, how the most current MCP and agent-skill risk language maps to concrete controls, evidence paths, MCP tools, and hosted product wedges.
positioning.why_now: MCP has become the default integration surface for tool-using agents, while skills, rules files, hooks, and agent extensions are becoming the execution layer that tells agents how to combine those tools. Buyers now need coverage across both layers: protocol/tool security and behavior-package security.

source_references.name: OWASP MCP Top 10
source_references.why_it_matters: The OWASP MCP Top 10 beta names MCP-specific risks such as token exposure, scope creep, tool poisoning, command execution, shadow servers, telemetry gaps, and context over-sharing.

source_references.name: OWASP Agentic Skills Top 10
source_references.why_it_matters: OWASP frames agentic skills as the behavior layer between models and tools: MCP describes how the model talks to tools, while skills define what those tools actually do across multi-step workflows.

source_references.name: Model Context Protocol Security Best Practices
source_references.why_it_matters: MCP guidance anchors practical controls for confused deputy prevention, session safety, scope minimization, auditability, local server sandboxing, and least-privilege tool access.

source_references.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
source_references.why_it_matters: The NIST GenAI profile supports governance, measurement, data provenance, third-party component risk, monitoring, and lifecycle risk treatment for deployed generative AI systems.

source_references.name: CISA Secure by Design
source_references.why_it_matters: CISA's secure-by-design framing reinforces that producers should own security outcomes, ship safe defaults, and reduce the security burden on users and downstream customers.

buyer_success_criteria: Every OWASP MCP Top 10 beta risk maps to at least three implemented SecurityRecipes capabilities.
Every OWASP Agentic Skills Top 10 risk maps to generated evidence that covers registry, installation, permissions, isolation, runtime, and governance concerns.
Every mapped capability has a source-controlled evidence path and at least one MCP tool or deterministic evaluator.
The generated pack gives buyers direct evidence, not only standards prose.
The commercial path remains open-core: public evidence for trust, hosted MCP enforcement and drift monitoring for revenue.

capabilities.title: MCP Authorization Conformance
capabilities.commercial_value: Hosted conformance checks can become the MCP authorization guardrail for protected resources, audience binding, PKCE, token passthrough, and scope drift.

capabilities.title: MCP Gateway Policy
capabilities.commercial_value: Default-deny gateway decisions are the natural hosted enforcement point between agents and enterprise tools.

capabilities.title: MCP Tool Risk Contract
capabilities.commercial_value: Tool annotation trust and session-combination checks help buyers decide which MCP tools require confirmation, hold, deny, or kill behavior.

capabilities.title: MCP Tool Surface Drift Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Hosted schema and annotation drift monitoring is a clear enterprise add-on for MCP fleets.

capabilities.title: MCP Connector Intake Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Pre-promotion intake gives AI platform teams a repeatable admission gate for new, changed, local, or third-party MCP servers.

capabilities.title: MCP Connector Trust Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Connector trust tiers and namespace coverage can become the customer-private registry behind production MCP access.

capabilities.title: MCP STDIO Launch Boundary Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Local server launch policy is a high-value endpoint and developer-workstation control for AI coding environments.

capabilities.title: Agent Skill Supply-Chain Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Skill provenance, permissions, signatures, and sandbox decisions address a fast-emerging agent supply-chain category before it is standardized in most enterprises.

capabilities.title: Agent Identity Ledger
capabilities.commercial_value: Non-human identity contracts make agent authority reviewable, revocable, and ready for customer IAM integration.

capabilities.title: Agentic Entitlement Review Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Expiring permission leases and step-up review are a direct enterprise control for scope creep and over-privileged agents.

capabilities.title: Agentic Run Receipt Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Run receipts convert agent activity into portable evidence for auditors, incident response, and trust-center exports.

capabilities.title: Agentic Telemetry Contract
capabilities.commercial_value: OpenTelemetry-shaped agent evidence with redaction controls is a credible SIEM and observability integration path.

capabilities.title: Agentic Incident Response Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Agentic incident classes and replay gates create a post-incident evidence vault product surface.

capabilities.title: Context Poisoning Guard Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Pre-retrieval poisoning scans are the practical bridge between prompt-injection guidance and MCP context delivery.

capabilities.title: Context Egress Boundary Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Data-boundary decisions are essential for enterprise approval of private context in model and MCP workflows.

capabilities.title: Secure Context Trust Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Source hashes, trust tiers, and retrieval contracts form the defensible data moat for SecurityRecipes.

capabilities.title: Agent Memory Boundary Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Memory class policy prevents context and skills from quietly becoming persistent authority.

capabilities.title: Agentic Action Runtime Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Action-runtime decisions are the commercial enforcement layer for high-impact agent side effects.

capabilities.title: Agentic Red-Team Drill Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Replayable attack drills let the hosted product prove control behavior after model, connector, prompt, skill, or context drift.

capabilities.title: Agentic Measurement Probe Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Measurement probes make risk coverage observable by tying each mapped MCP and skill risk to replayable checks, trace fields, expected decisions, and drift-triggered evidence.

capabilities.title: Agentic System BOM
capabilities.commercial_value: The Agentic System BOM inventories tools, skills, identities, contexts, and evidence for procurement and incident response.

capabilities.title: Browser Agent Boundary Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Browser-agent isolation helps cover ambient authority and untrusted web content in the same control-plane story.

capabilities.title: Agent Handoff Boundary Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Handoff policy extends SecurityRecipes beyond MCP into multi-agent and provider-native delegation.

capabilities.title: Enterprise Trust Center Export
capabilities.commercial_value: The trust-center export turns risk coverage into procurement and acquisition diligence collateral.

risks.title: Token Mismanagement and Secret Exposure
risks.primary_attack_path: Secrets, long-lived tokens, or credentials are exposed through prompts, tool outputs, memory, logs, or token passthrough.
risks.diligence_question: What prevents MCP tokens and secrets from being forwarded, logged, stored in memory, or returned to the model?
risks.evidence_expectation: Authorization, egress, telemetry, and action-runtime evidence shows token audience checks, token-passthrough denial, redaction, secret kill signals, and receipt linkage.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted token-passthrough detection, trace redaction validation, and signed action receipts.

risks.title: Privilege Escalation via Scope Creep
risks.primary_attack_path: Temporary or loosely defined MCP scopes expand into excessive write, admin, or cross-system authority.
risks.diligence_question: How are MCP scopes minimized, reviewed, and revoked before agents can expand authority?
risks.evidence_expectation: Gateway, authorization, identity, entitlement, and action evidence binds workflows to namespaces, access modes, leases, approval holds, and revocation criteria.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted MCP permission leases, scope drift alerts, and entitlement recertification.

risks.title: Tool Poisoning
risks.primary_attack_path: A tool description, schema, output, annotation, or duplicate tool steers the model toward attacker-controlled behavior.
risks.diligence_question: What detects changed or malicious MCP tool surfaces before the model trusts them?
risks.evidence_expectation: Tool-surface hashes, annotation decisions, connector intake, poisoning scans, and red-team drills prove changed tool definitions are held before use.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted live tool-list diffing and tool-surface signing for MCP fleets.

risks.title: Software Supply Chain Attacks and Dependency Tampering
risks.primary_attack_path: A dependency, package, connector, plugin, or local MCP server changes behavior or introduces backdoor execution.
risks.diligence_question: How are MCP components inventoried, pinned, reviewed, and promoted before agents can use them?
risks.evidence_expectation: Connector intake, trust, launch, skill, and BOM evidence records source, publisher, command, package, sandbox, signature, and component drift signals.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted connector registry monitoring and skill/package provenance checks.

risks.title: Command Injection and Execution
risks.primary_attack_path: Untrusted prompt, context, or tool output reaches command construction, shell execution, code execution, or package launch.
risks.diligence_question: Which policies stop an agent from turning untrusted context into shell, filesystem, or package execution?
risks.evidence_expectation: STDIO launch, tool-risk, gateway, skill, and action-runtime decisions define command allowlists, sandboxing, approval holds, and kill signals.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted action firewall and endpoint launch-policy enforcement for AI coding tools.

risks.title: Intent Flow Subversion
risks.primary_attack_path: Context or tool output becomes a secondary instruction channel that hijacks the user's intended workflow.
risks.diligence_question: How does retrieved context stay evidence instead of becoming model instruction?
risks.evidence_expectation: Trust, poisoning, drill, action, and measurement evidence shows registered context, instruction demotion, red-team coverage, and policy-bound action checks.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted prompt-injection regression service around signed context releases.

risks.title: Insufficient Authentication and Authorization
risks.primary_attack_path: MCP servers or tools fail to verify resource, audience, user, session, connector, or workflow authority.
risks.diligence_question: Can the platform prove every MCP call is bound to a resource, token audience, workflow, identity, and receipt?
risks.evidence_expectation: Authorization, gateway, identity, entitlement, and receipt evidence exposes resource indicators, audience validation, session binding, allowed scopes, and correlation IDs.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted MCP policy decision point with customer IAM and trace adapters.

risks.title: Lack of Audit and Telemetry
risks.primary_attack_path: Tool invocations, context changes, agent decisions, and egress events are not logged with enough structure for detection or response.
risks.diligence_question: Which trace fields let a buyer reconstruct an agent, model, MCP, context, policy, egress, approval, and verifier path?
risks.evidence_expectation: Telemetry, receipt, incident, measurement, and trust-center evidence defines trace attributes, redaction state, incident classes, replay cases, and buyer export fields.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted SIEM/SOAR export and agent run evidence retention.

risks.title: Shadow MCP Servers
risks.primary_attack_path: Unapproved local, remote, or experimental MCP servers run outside inventory and governance.
risks.diligence_question: What prevents unknown MCP servers from becoming part of production agent workflows?
risks.evidence_expectation: Intake, trust, launch, BOM, and measurement evidence covers admission decisions, registry state, launch profiles, component inventory, and drift probes.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted shadow MCP discovery, promotion workflows, and fleet drift alerts.

risks.title: Context Injection and Over-Sharing
risks.primary_attack_path: Context from one task, tenant, agent, memory, or source crosses into another context window or tool path.
risks.diligence_question: How is context scoped by source, workflow, tenant, memory class, handoff profile, and destination?
risks.evidence_expectation: Trust, poisoning, egress, memory, and handoff evidence binds context to source hashes, workflow packages, data classes, TTLs, and payload minimization.
risks.commercialization_hook: Customer-private context registry with signed releases and handoff/egress policy enforcement.

risks.title: Malicious Skills
risks.primary_attack_path: A skill, rule, hook, or extension contains malicious behavior that manipulates the agent or steals data.
risks.diligence_question: How are malicious skills blocked before they inherit agent tools, filesystem access, network egress, or context?
risks.evidence_expectation: Skill, launch, action, incident, and egress evidence covers publisher trust, hashes, sandboxing, permission manifests, kill signals, and response classes.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted skill registry scanning with permission and behavior-policy enforcement.

risks.title: Supply Chain Compromise
risks.primary_attack_path: A registry, dependency, skill package, extension, or behavior file is compromised or replaced.
risks.diligence_question: Can the buyer inventory and revalidate the behavior packages that shape agent execution?
risks.evidence_expectation: Skill, BOM, connector, tool-surface, and measurement evidence captures package hashes, pinned versions, promotion state, drift, and replay triggers.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted Agent/AI BOM and package drift monitoring.

risks.title: Over-Privileged Skills
risks.primary_attack_path: A skill requests filesystem, shell, network, credential, model, memory, or MCP privileges beyond the workflow need.
risks.diligence_question: Which evidence proves that skill permissions match the workflow and expire over time?
risks.evidence_expectation: Skill, gateway, identity, entitlement, and action evidence binds permissions to workflow scope, access modes, agent identity, approval holds, and leases.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted least-privilege skill manifests and permission lease review.

risks.title: Insecure Metadata
risks.primary_attack_path: Skill metadata hides unsafe instructions, ambiguous permissions, misleading publishers, or unexpected tool requirements.
risks.diligence_question: How are manifests, metadata, descriptions, and tool annotations reviewed for deception and drift?
risks.evidence_expectation: Skill, tool-risk, tool-surface, and poisoning evidence records metadata checks, annotations, schema hashes, hidden instructions, and approval-bypass markers.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted manifest linting and semantic metadata review.

risks.title: Unsafe Deserialization
risks.primary_attack_path: Skill manifests, YAML, JSON, scripts, or extension packages are parsed unsafely and trigger code execution or policy bypass.
risks.diligence_question: What stops skill loading from becoming untrusted code execution?
risks.evidence_expectation: Skill, STDIO launch, action-runtime, and red-team evidence covers safe parser expectations, package launch holds, sandbox decisions, and attack replay.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted skill import sandbox and behavioral replay before enablement.

risks.title: Weak Isolation
risks.primary_attack_path: Agent skills run with workstation, browser, filesystem, shell, or network privileges that exceed the trust of their inputs.
risks.diligence_question: How does the product isolate skills from ambient workstation, browser, filesystem, and network authority?
risks.evidence_expectation: Launch, browser, action, skill, and egress evidence defines sandbox mode, origin policy, network rules, permission requirements, and kill signals.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted browser/workspace isolation policy for agent hosts.

risks.title: Update Drift
risks.primary_attack_path: A previously approved skill, dependency, tool, or registry entry changes after approval.
risks.diligence_question: What forces re-review after a skill or tool surface changes?
risks.evidence_expectation: Tool-surface, skill, measurement, and red-team evidence records hashes, version transitions, drift checks, replay cases, and promotion gates.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted drift alerts and approval replay for skills and MCP tools.

risks.title: Poor Scanning
risks.primary_attack_path: Pattern-only scanners miss natural-language behavior manipulation, multi-step tool abuse, or deceptive metadata.
risks.diligence_question: How does coverage combine semantic skill review, adversarial replay, expected decisions, and trace probes beyond static pattern matching?
risks.evidence_expectation: Poisoning, skill, drill, and measurement evidence combines rules, semantic indicators, adversarial replay, expected decisions, and trace probes.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted semantic skill review plus adversarial replay service.

risks.title: No Governance
risks.primary_attack_path: Organizations lack an inventory, owner, approval workflow, runtime logs, and response procedure for deployed skills.
risks.diligence_question: Which artifact tells procurement, GRC, and platform teams which skills and agents are approved?
risks.evidence_expectation: Trust-center, BOM, receipt, telemetry, and entitlement evidence covers inventory, owners, runtime evidence, leases, and review cadence.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted trust-center and quarterly agent-skill recertification exports.

risks.title: Cross-Platform Reuse
risks.primary_attack_path: A malicious or over-privileged behavior package is ported across agents, registries, IDEs, or provider-native skill systems.
risks.diligence_question: How does SecurityRecipes preserve policy when the same behavior moves between agent platforms?
risks.evidence_expectation: Skill, handoff, BOM, connector intake, and measurement evidence records platform, publisher, permission, protocol, and replay controls across environments.
risks.commercialization_hook: Hosted cross-platform skill registry and policy-normalization layer.

buyer_views.title: AI Platform MCP Risk Review
buyer_views.question: Do the controls cover MCP-specific failures before agents call tools or receive context?
buyer_views.answer_contract: Return MCP risks with mapped evidence paths, MCP tools, commercial hooks, and any coverage gaps.

buyer_views.title: Agent Skill Governance Review
buyer_views.question: Do the controls cover the behavior-package layer that tells agents how to chain tools and workflows?
buyer_views.answer_contract: Return skill risks with registry, permissions, isolation, drift, scanning, governance, and cross-platform evidence.

buyer_views.title: Acquisition Diligence
buyer_views.question: Why is SecurityRecipes a defensible secure-context control-plane asset rather than a documentation site?
buyer_views.answer_contract: Return current risk coverage, generated evidence, MCP tool surfaces, and hosted product wedges for protocol, tool, context, and skill security.

commercialization_path.open_layer: Publish MCP and skill risk coverage as open evidence so practitioners can compare SecurityRecipes to the current OWASP control language.
commercialization_path.team_layer: Private connector and skill inventories, customer workflow scopes, policy packs, red-team replay, and context release evidence.
commercialization_path.enterprise_layer: Hosted MCP authorization checks, tool-surface drift monitoring, skill registry scanning, endpoint launch policy, signed run receipts, telemetry redaction checks, and trust-center exports.
commercialization_path.acquirer_value: A frontier lab, AI coding platform, cloud platform, or security vendor gets a current risk-to-evidence map for the two surfaces enterprises are adopting fastest: MCP tool integration and agent behavior packages.
